metadata.title:
SEE PLAN TO LAY


BLAME ON LABOR

pages.raw_text:
In placing the Alaska-Yukon-
Pacific exposition on the unfair
list the federation of labor dele
gates were influenced by the belief
that the exposition management
was deliberately forcing them to
such action. A committee was APP
pointed to draft letter to the pub
lie explaining the attitude of the
unions toward the fair. II was
stated there was reason to believe
that the financial conditions pre-
vailing were such as to make 1t a
difficult matter for the fair to real
lie on the lake front property the
last legislature agreed to sell to
create a fund for the fair.


1t is also the belief of many on
the coast that the United States
government would be slow to APP
propriate money in view of the fail
ure of the jamestown exposition.
Under these circumstances the la
Dor men feel that they are being
made to bear the blame for the fall
ure Of the project to which they
originally subscribed money liber-
ally,
